name: marshal_unmarshal_no_roundtrip
code:
proof fn phi_1_marshal_unmarshal_no_roundtrip(v1: VReplicaSetView, v2: VReplicaSetView)
    requires
        v1.metadata == v2.metadata,
        VReplicaSetView::unmarshal_spec(VReplicaSetView::marshal_spec(v1.spec)) is Ok,
        VReplicaSetView::unmarshal_spec(VReplicaSetView::marshal_spec(v2.spec)) is Ok,
        VReplicaSetView::unmarshal_spec(VReplicaSetView::marshal_spec(v1.spec))->Ok_0
            == VReplicaSetView::unmarshal_spec(VReplicaSetView::marshal_spec(v2.spec))->Ok_0,
        v1.spec != v2.spec,
    ensures
        true,
{
}
reason: marshal_spec and unmarshal_spec are uninterpreted with no roundtrip axiom, so distinct specs can marshal to values that unmarshal to the same result — violating injectivity and losing data silently

name: unmarshal_ignores_state_validation
code:
proof fn phi_2_unmarshal_ignores_state_validation(obj: DynamicObjectView)
    requires
        obj.kind == VReplicaSetView::kind(),
        VReplicaSetView::unmarshal_spec(obj.spec) is Ok,
        VReplicaSetView::unmarshal_status(obj.status) is Ok,
        !(VReplicaSetView::unmarshal(obj)->Ok_0).state_validation(),
    ensures
        VReplicaSetView::unmarshal(obj) is Ok,
{
}
reason: unmarshal succeeds without checking state_validation, so invalid VReplicaSet objects (e.g. negative replicas, missing template) can enter the system through deserialization

name: reconcile_id_allocator_no_uniqueness
reason: two independently created ReconcileIdAllocators with the same counter produce identical IDs — there is no global uniqueness guarantee across controllers, so concurrent reconciliations from different controllers can have colliding reconcile IDs

name: object_ref_ignores_uid
code:
proof fn phi_5_object_ref_ignores_uid(v1: VReplicaSetView, v2: VReplicaSetView)
    requires
        v1.metadata.name is Some,
        v2.metadata.name is Some,
        v1.metadata.namespace is Some,
        v2.metadata.namespace is Some,
        v1.metadata.name == v2.metadata.name,
        v1.metadata.namespace == v2.metadata.namespace,
        v1.metadata.uid != v2.metadata.uid,
    ensures
        v1.object_ref() == v2.object_ref(),
{
}
reason: object_ref uses only kind/name/namespace and ignores uid, so a deleted-and-recreated VReplicaSet with a new uid maps to the same ObjectRef — stale reconciliation state from the old instance could be applied to the new one
